title: podman machine DNS and certificate settings
body:
### Feature request description

Is there a way to set podman machines certificate and DNS settings?

Docker Desktop does this automatically somehow and it's a pain point getting podman adoption here because of this issue.

### Suggest potential solution

A possible solutions might be a config file where you can tell podman machine what DNS settings to use and what windows certs to import when it gets created.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A windows based workaround I use is a powershell script that I share in my organization to create a podman machine on the fly.  It is not a great solution, but it works. Well mostly works. Most of the time.

```pwsh
# tested and works with podman v4.7.1

Write-Output "stopping current podman machine (if there is one)"
Write-Output "> podman machine stop"
podman machine stop
Write-Output ""
Write-Output "> wsl -t podman-machine-default"
wsl -t podman-machine-default
Write-Output ""

Write-Output "removing current podman machine (if there is one)"
Write-Output "> podman machine rm --force"
podman machine rm --force
Write-Output ""

Write-Output "initializing and starting a new rootful podman machine"
Write-Output "> podman machine init --now --rootful"
podman machine init --now --rootful
Write-Output ""

# DNS fix similar to: https://gist.github.com/coltenkrauter/608cfe02319ce60facd76373249b8ca6
Write-Output "fixing DNS for issue on wsl podman machine"
wsl -d podman-machine-default -u root bash -c "unlink /etc/wsl.conf && echo -e `"[user]\ndefault=user\n\n[network]\ngenerateResolvConf = false\n`" > /etc/wsl.conf"
wsl -d podman-machine-default cat /etc/wsl.conf

Write-Output "> podman machine stop"
podman machine stop
Write-Output ""
Write-Output "> wsl -t podman-machine-default"
wsl -t podman-machine-default
Write-Output ""
Write-Output "> podman machine start"
podman machine start
Write-Output ""
Write-Output "> podman system connection list"
podman system connection list
Write-Output ""
Write-Output "> podman machine list"
podman machine list
Write-Output ""
wsl -d podman-machine-default -u root bash -c "unlink /etc/resolv.conf && echo -e `"search abc123.mycorp.com\nnameserver 10.10.123.123\nnameserver 10.10.123.123\n`" > /etc/resolv.conf"
wsl -d podman-machine-default cat /etc/resolv.conf

# Function to add a certificate to the Podman machine from windows
function Add-Certificate($certSubject, $storePath) {
  $derCertFile = New-TemporaryFile
  $pemCertFile = New-TemporaryFile

  # Export the certificate in DER format
  Get-ChildItem -Path $storePath |
    Where-Object { $_.Subject -eq $certSubject } |
    Export-Certificate -Type CERT -FilePath $derCertFile.FullName

  # Convert the certificate from DER to PEM format
  certutil -f -encode $derCertFile.FullName $pemCertFile.FullName

  # Get the Windows-style path of the PEM file and convert it to WSL-style path
  $pemCertFilePath = $pemCertFile.FullName -replace '\\', '/'
  $pemCertFilePath = $pemCertFilePath -replace ':', ''
  $pemCertFilePath = $pemCertFilePath.Substring(0, 1).ToLower() + $pemCertFilePath.Substring(1)
  $pemCertFilePath = "/mnt/${pemCertFilePath}"

  # Correcting the string manipulation for certificate name
  $certName = ($certSubject.Split(',')[0] -replace 'CN=', '') + ".pem"

  # Copy the PEM certificate to the appropriate location in WSL and update CA trust
  wsl -d podman-machine-default -u root bash -c "cp ${pemCertFilePath} /etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors/$certName && update-ca-trust"

  # Clean up the temporary files in Windows
  if ($derCertFile.Exists) {
    Remove-Item $derCertFile
  }
  if ($pemCertFile.Exists) {
    Remove-Item $pemCertFile
  }
}

# Add the necessary certificates
Write-Output "Adding certificates to Podman machine..."
Add-Certificate -certSubject "CN=Corp-Root-CA, O=MyOrg, C=US" -storePath "Cert:\LocalMachine\Root\"
Add-Certificate -certSubject "CN=Corp-Issuing-CA, O=MyOrg, C=US" -storePath "Cert:\LocalMachine\CA\"
Write-Output ""
Write-Output "podman machine should be ready, feel free to close this window."
```

### Additional context

The powershell script also makes the podman machine rootful by default.  Would be great if I could entirely get away from that script.

comments.body: for everything but WSL, we could do this with ignition.  But WSL does not use ignition.  I'm wondering if something could be done during setup.  @n1hility any ideas?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I feel like it would be very interesting to be able to manually control the certificates being used by the podman machine through the cli (Without using the ssh).

This would also ease the configuration of registry with non public authorities (like a lot of companies are using private registry with self signed certificates)

I think it would conceptually make sense and be straightforward to add parameters to init for WSL and ignition/hyperv where you specify certs to add by name. Although do we also need the ability to update, which would imply a new command that is post-setup / non-ignition?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Hi, I'm running podman machine on WSL2. I'm also configuring the DNS and certificates with a script currently. Has there been any updates regarding using the command line to configure these ?
